candidate_summary: Map Coanda effect (slot-blown jet keeps flow attached to a deflected flap via pressure asymmetry) to LLM reasoning: inject anchor/attention-sink tokens at branching CoT steps to keep attention 'attached' to correct reasoning trajectories, enlarging the basin of stability for correct CoT paths.

results.title: Coanda effect (Wikipedia)
results.judge_reason: Pure source-domain reference about Coanda effect; no LLM-side keyword overlap and not directly used in the candidate's LLM mapping.

results.title: Fundamentals in Coanda Flap Design
results.judge_reason: Source-domain engineering reference on Coanda flap design; contains no LLM-side keywords or mapping content.

results.title: Design and Experimental Characterization of an Actuation System for Internally Blown Coanda Flap
results.judge_reason: Experimental aerodynamics paper for blown Coanda flap; no LLM-side content present.

results.title: When Attention Sink Emerges (ICLR 2025)
results.judge_reason: Directly explains attention sink as strong attention to initial tokens emerging in pretraining, supporting the anchor-token mechanism but not the Coanda mapping itself.

results.title: Structural Origin of Attention Sink: Variance Discrepancy, Super Neurons, Dimension Disparity
results.judge_reason: Discusses sinks anchoring at position zero and structural origin via causal masking, closely tied to the anchor-token framing.

results.title: Attention Sinks and Compression Valleys in LLMs are Two Sides of the Same Coin
results.judge_reason: Names the 'selective refinement' phase and ties sinks to massive activations in the residual stream, matching multiple LLM-side keywords of the candidate.

results.title: ROLL Attention Illuminates LLM Reasoning: Preplan-and-Anchor
results.judge_reason: Preplan-and-Anchor frames anchor tokens as organizing downstream reasoning, directly aligned with the candidate's anchor-injection-at-branch-points proposal.

results.title: Efficient Streaming Language Models with Attention Sinks
results.judge_reason: Foundational StreamingLLM attention-sink paper supporting the sink-token literature cited but not the Coanda mapping itself.

verdict: Candidate is partially supported: LLM-side anchor/attention-sink/residual-stream/reasoning literature (3 hits) corroborates the mechanism being invoked, while source-domain Coanda references are not LLM-relevant by the keyword/cos/judge rule. The cross-domain mapping itself (Coanda jet -> anchor-token injection at CoT branch points as basin-stability device) remains novel and unhit, consistent with the candidate's novelty claim.
